title: Zombie Scavenger Atomic Wasteland

narrative: job-first genre hook: a robot scavenger enters a readable cleanup mission before romance takes over
compressed worldbuilding through repeated icons: seaside resort, dead town, robot cowboy, ostrich mount, mannequin, orbital escape
black-humor turn from zombie combat into lonely mannequin romance and sentimental ritual
camera: ultra-wide cinematic frames with ocean glare, luxury architecture, and dead-street scale
hero low angles, centered silhouettes, diagonal action staging, and prop-to-location reveals
smooth generated camera paths: slow push, lateral tracking, action follow, aerial-feeling opens
editing: under-four-minute story economy with roughly 90 high-contrast scene-change candidates in a 213.5 second sample
action bursts alternate with deadpan LED-face pauses and romantic absurdity
cuts motivated by task progress, location reveal, title-card emphasis, or comic reversal
color_light: atom-punk retro-futurism with cyan ocean glare, sun-bleached orange, rust metal, and red title typography
dust, chrome, cracked concrete, tropical resort glass, zombie residue, and weathered plastics
practical LED face color as character emotion and technical workaround
sound: sound-on genre impact is implied by AAC audio track and combat choreography
music and effects should support gunfire, mechanical motion, surf/wind, crowd threat, and romantic lift
no subtitle track found in Bilibili metadata; visual storytelling should remain legible without dialogue
performance: robot face uses LED/pixel expression instead of fragile AI human micro-expression
mechanical body comedy, cowboy swagger, and lonely restrained tenderness
animal/vehicle micro-gags give the world memory and charm
platform: strong one-sentence share hook: robot cowboy on an ostrich clears zombies and falls for a mannequin
Bilibili, Douyin, Reddit, and X-friendly genre readability despite ultra-wide framing
comment culture frames it as movie-like AI rather than a tool demo

usage_constraints: Use as a transferable taste grammar, not a shot-for-shot remake of Zombie Scavenger.
Do not copy the robot cowboy, ostrich mount, mannequin romance, exact ending, or creator identity as a package.
Avoid using living creator name or platform account as a style prompt.
For new work, change the job, companion object, vehicle/animal logic, setting geography, and emotional payoff.
